docs.title: Notionary
docs.text:
Transform complex Notion API interactions into simple, Pythonic code. Whether you're building AI agents, automating workflows, or creating dynamic content, Notionary makes it effortless.

# Find a page and update it with rich content
page = await NotionPage.from_page_name("My Project")
await page.replace_content("""
# 🚀 Project Overview

!> [💡] This page was created programmatically!

+++ Implementation Details
| Built with Notionary's intuitive Python API
| Rich Markdown support with custom extensions
""")

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Object-Oriented Design - Clean, intuitive classes for Pages, Databases, and Workspaces
  • Rich Markdown Support - Transform extended Markdown (callouts, toggles, columns) into Notion blocks
  • Smart Discovery - Find pages and databases by name with fuzzy matching
  • Async-First Architecture - Built for modern Python with full async/await supportw
  • AI-Ready Integration - Generate LLM system prompts for AI content creation

Build something amazing with Notionary! 🚀

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
We'd love your help making Notionary even better! 🚀

Whether you want to:

  • Fix a bug you found
  • Add a cool new feature
  • Improve the documentation
  • Share an idea or suggestion

All contributions are very welcome!

Feel free to open an issue, submit a pull request, or just start a discussion. No contribution is too small - from fixing typos to adding major features, we appreciate it all.

Thanks for helping make Notionary awesome! 🙏

docs.title: Features
docs.text: Notionary provides a comprehensive set of features for automating your Notion workspace with Python.

docs.title: Database Operations
docs.text:
Connect to databases using fuzzy name matching - no need for complex URLs or IDs.

# Find database by name (fuzzy matching works!)
db = await NotionDatabase.from_database_name("My Projects")
print(f"Connected to: {db.title}")

docs.title: Page Creation & Management
docs.text:
Create pages in databases and set properties programmatically.

# Create a new page in the database
page = await db.create_blank_page()
await page.set_title("🆕 New Project Entry")

# Set database properties
await page.set_property_value_by_name("Status", "In Progress")
await page.set_property_value_by_name("Priority", "High")

docs.title: Advanced Filtering & Querying
docs.text:
Query database pages with powerful filters and iterate through results.

# Query recent pages with filters
async for i, page in enumerate(db.iter_pages_with_filter(
    db.create_filter().with_created_last_n_days(7)
), start=1):
    print(f"{i}. {page.emoji_icon or '📄'} {page.title}")

docs.title: Page Management
docs.text:
Find pages by name without needing exact matches or URLs.

# Find page by name (fuzzy matching supported)
page = await NotionPage.from_page_name("Meeting Notes")

docs.title: Page Metadata Control
docs.text:
Full control over page appearance and metadata.

# Update page metadata
await page.set_title("🚀 Updated Project Title")
await page.set_emoji_icon("🚀")
await page.set_random_gradient_cover()

docs.title: Content Management
docs.text:
Add, replace, or append content using rich Markdown syntax.

# Replace entire page content
await page.replace_content(markdown_content)

# Append new content with optional divider
await page.append_markdown(markdown_content, append_divider=True)

# Get existing content as markdown
text_content = await page.get_text_content()

docs.title: Workspace Discovery
docs.text:
Discover all databases in your Notion workspace.

workspace = NotionWorkspace()

# List all databases
databases = await workspace.list_all_databases()
for db in databases:
    print(f"🗃️ {db.title} - {db.url}")

docs.title: Page Search
docs.text:
Search for pages across your entire workspace.

# Search for specific pages
pages = await workspace.search_pages("meeting notes", limit=5)
for page in pages:
    print(f"📄 {page.title} - {page.url}")

docs.title: Rich Markdown Extensions
docs.text: Notionary extends standard Markdown with Notion-specific elements.

docs.title: Basic Formatting
docs.text:
Standard Markdown with full support for text formatting, lists, and links.

**Bold text**, _italic text_, `inline code`

- Bulleted lists

1. Numbered lists
2. For ordered content

[Links](https://example.com) and inline `code variables`

docs.title: Callouts
docs.text:
Create eye-catching callouts with custom emojis.

!> [💡] This is a tip callout with a light bulb emoji
!> [⚠️] Warning: Important information here
!> [✅] Success: Operation completed successfully

docs.title: Toggles
docs.text:
Create collapsible content sections.

+++ Click to expand details
++ Click to expand details
| Hidden content goes here
| Use pipe syntax for toggle content
| Perfect for hiding implementation details

docs.title: Multi-Column Layouts
docs.text:
Create side-by-side content with column layouts.

::: columns
::: column

## Left Column

Content for the left side
:::
::: column

## Right Column

Content for the right side
:::
:::

docs.title: Code Blocks with Captions
docs.text:
Syntax-highlighted code blocks with optional captions.

```python
def hello_world():
    print("Hello from Notionary!")
```

Caption: Basic Python function example


### Tables
Standard Markdown tables with full formatting support.

```markdown
| Feature | Status | Priority |
|---------|--------|----------|
| API Integration | Complete | High |
| Documentation | In Progress | Medium |

docs.title: Media Embeds
docs.text:
Embed videos, images, and other media content.

# YouTube videos

<embed:Python Tutorial>(https://youtube.com/watch?v=...)

# Audio files

$[Podcast Episode](https://example.com/audio.mp3)

# Images with captions

![Image Caption](https://example.com/image.jpg)

docs.title: Async-First Architecture
docs.text:
Built from the ground up with async/await for high performance.

import asyncio

async def main():
    # All operations are async
    page = await NotionPage.from_page_name("My Page")
    await page.replace_content("# Hello World")

    # Efficient batch operations
    async for page in db.iter_pages():
        await page.set_property_value_by_name("Status", "Updated")

asyncio.run(main())

docs.title: Error Handling & Retries
docs.text:
Robust error handling with automatic retries for network issues.

try:
    page = await NotionPage.from_page_name("Non-existent Page")
except Exception as e:
    print(f"Page not found: {e}")

docs.title: AI-Ready Integration
docs.text:
Generate system prompts for AI agents to create Notion content.

from notionary import BlockRegistryBuilder

# Create a registry with all elements
registry = BlockRegistryBuilder.create_registry()

# Generate LLM system prompt
llm_prompt = registry.get_notion_markdown_syntax_prompt()
print(llm_prompt)  # Use this to teach AI agents about Notionary's syntax

docs.title: AI Content Creation
docs.text:
Perfect foundation for AI agents that generate Notion content.

# AI agents can use Notionary's extended Markdown
ai_generated_content = """
# AI-Generated Report

!> [🤖] This content was generated by an AI agent using Notionary!

++ Technical Details
+++ Technical Details
| The AI used natural language processing to analyze data
| and automatically generate this structured report.
"""

await page.replace_content(ai_generated_content)

docs.title: Custom Block Registry
docs.text:
Customize which Markdown elements are supported.

from notionary import BlockRegistryBuilder

# Create custom registry with only needed elements
custom_registry = (
    BlockRegistryBuilder()
    .with_headings()
    .with_callouts()
    .with_toggles()
    .with_columns()
    .build()
)

# Apply to a page
page.block_registry = custom_registry

docs.title: Type Safety
docs.text:
Full type hints for better IDE support and code reliability.

from notionary import NotionPage, NotionDatabase

# IDE will provide full autocompletion and type checking
page: NotionPage = await NotionPage.from_page_name("My Page")
db: NotionDatabase = await NotionDatabase.from_database_name("Projects")

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Welcome to Notionary! This guide will get you up and running in just a few minutes.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Install Notionary using pip:

pip install notionary

Requirements:

  • Python 3.8 or higher
  • A Notion account with workspace access

docs.title: Setup Your Notion Integration
docs.text: Before using Notionary, you need to create a Notion integration and get your API token.

docs.title: Step 1: Create a Notion Integration
docs.text:
1. Go to notion.so/profile/integrations
  2. Click "+ New integration"
  3. Give your integration a name (e.g., "My Notionary App")
  4. Select the workspace you want to use
  5. Click "Submit"

docs.title: Step 2: Get Your Integration Token
docs.text: After creating the integration, you'll see an "Internal Integration Token". Copy this token - you'll need it next.

docs.title: Step 3: Set Environment Variable
docs.text:
Create a .env file in your project directory:

NOTION_SECRET=your_integration_token_here

Or set it directly in your environment:

export NOTION_SECRET=your_integration_token_here

docs.title: Step 4: Share Pages with Your Integration
docs.text:
Your integration needs access to the pages/databases you want to work with:

  1. Open the Notion page or database you want to use
  2. Click "Share" in the top-right corner
  3. Click "Invite" and search for your integration name
  4. Select your integration and click "Invite"

docs.title: Your First Notionary Script
docs.text:
Let's create a simple script that updates a Notion page:

import asyncio
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()

from notionary import NotionPage

async def main():
    # Find a page by name (make sure it exists and is shared with your integration)
    page = await NotionPage.from_page_name("My Test Page")

    # Update the page with rich content
    content = """
    # 🚀 Hello from Notionary!

    !> [💡] This content was generated programmatically using Python!

    ## What you can do:
    - Create and update pages
    - Manage database entries
    - Use rich Markdown with custom extensions
    - Build AI-powered content generators

    +++ Click to see more details
    | Notionary makes it incredibly easy to work with Notion's API.
    | No more complex JSON structures - just write Markdown!
    """

    await page.replace_content(content)
    print(f"✅ Page updated successfully!")
    print(f"🔗 View it here: {page.url}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

Run your script:

python your_script.py

docs.title: Working with Databases
docs.text:
Here's how to work with Notion databases:

import asyncio
from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()

from notionary import NotionDatabase

async def main():
    # Connect to a database by name
    db = await NotionDatabase.from_database_name("My Projects")

    # Create a new page in the database
    page = await db.create_blank_page()
    await page.set_title("🆕 New Project")
    await page.set_property_value_by_name("Status", "Planning")

    # Query recent pages
    print("Recent projects:")
    async for i, page in enumerate(db.iter_pages_with_filter(
        db.create_filter().with_created_last_n_days(30)
    ), start=1):
        print(f"{i}. {page.title}")

asyncio.run(main())

Happy automating! 🚀
